# sft command reference

Authoritative list of every (non-hidden) `sft` command and its flags.
Every read-side command supports `--json`; agents should always pass it when output will be parsed.

## `sft browse`

Open an interactive TUI browser for a .safetensors file.

Launches a full-screen terminal UI for exploring tensor names,
shapes, dtypes, and values. Navigate with arrow keys, filter with '/'.

Examples:
  sft browse model.safetensors
  sft model.safetensors              (shorthand ‚Äî browse is the default)

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required

## `sft cast`

Cast tensor dtypes in a .safetensors file.

Convert all (or selected) tensors to a different dtype. Useful for
quantising models to fp16/bf16 or promoting to fp32.

Examples:
  sft cast model.safetensors --dtype fp16
  sft cast model.safetensors --dtype bf16 --exclude='*.norm.*'
  sft cast model.safetensors --dtype fp32 --include='**.weight' --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--dtype` ‚Äî option, required. Target dtype (bf16, bfloat16, float16, float32, float64, fp16, fp32, fp64).
- `--include` ‚Äî option. Only cast tensors matching this glob pattern.
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option. Skip tensors matching this glob pattern.
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.{dtype}.safetensors).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show what would be cast without writing.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft cat`

Merge multiple .safetensors files into one.

Concatenates tensors from all input files. Fails on name collisions
unless --allow-duplicates is set (last file wins).

Examples:
  sft cat shard_01.safetensors shard_02.safetensors -o merged.safetensors
  sft cat *.safetensors --allow-duplicates
  sft cat part1.safetensors part2.safetensors --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `FILES` ‚Äî argument, required (repeatable)
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output file path (default: merged.safetensors in CWD).
- `--allow-duplicates` ‚Äî flag. On tensor name collision, keep the version from the last file.
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show what the merged file would contain without writing.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft check`

Validate a .safetensors file's integrity and check for NaN/Inf values.

Reads the full file to verify all tensors are loadable. Exits with
code 1 if corruption or bad values are detected.

Examples:
  sft check model.safetensors
  sft check model.safetensors --skip-values
  sft check model.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--skip-values` ‚Äî flag. Skip NaN/Inf scan (faster for huge files).
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft convert`

Convert a PyTorch checkpoint file to safetensors format.

Supports .pt, .pth, and .bin files. Requires PyTorch to be installed.

Examples:
  sft convert model.pt
  sft convert checkpoint.bin -o model.safetensors
  sft convert model.pth --dtype fp16

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.safetensors).
- `--dtype` ‚Äî option. Cast tensors to this dtype during conversion (e.g. fp16, fp32).
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft diff`

Compare two .safetensors files and show structural or value differences.

Shows added/removed tensors, shape changes, and dtype changes.
Use --delta for numerical comparison (L2 norm, cosine similarity).

Examples:
  sft diff base.safetensors finetuned.safetensors
  sft diff v1.safetensors v2.safetensors --delta
  sft diff a.safetensors b.safetensors --include='**.weight' --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE_A` ‚Äî argument, required
- `FILE_B` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--delta` ‚Äî flag. Compute value-level differences (L2 norm, cosine similarity).
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as machine-readable JSON.
- `--include` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern to include tensors.
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern to exclude tensors.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft info`

Print a non-interactive summary of a .safetensors file.

Shows file size, tensor count, dtype distribution, and metadata.

Examples:
  sft info model.safetensors
  sft info model.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft lora add`

Combine LoRA adapters via weighted task arithmetic.

Adds multiple LoRA deltas together with optional per-adapter weights.

Examples:
  sft lora add style.safetensors content.safetensors -o combined.safetensors
  sft lora add a.safetensors b.safetensors -w 0.7 -w 0.3
  sft lora add a.safetensors b.safetensors --rank 16 --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `FILES` ‚Äî argument, required (repeatable)
- `--weights`/`-w` ‚Äî option. Weight for each LoRA (default: equal weights).
- `--rank`/`-r` ‚Äî option. Output rank (default: same as input).
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: combined.safetensors).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show what would be combined without writing.

## `sft lora compat`

Check if a LoRA adapter is compatible with a base model.

Verifies that target modules exist in the base model and shapes align.

Examples:
  sft lora compat base.safetensors adapter.safetensors

**Parameters:**

- `BASE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `ADAPTER` ‚Äî argument, required

## `sft lora convert`

Convert a LoRA adapter between Kohya and PEFT naming conventions.

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--to` ‚Äî option. Target format ('kohya' or 'peft'). Default: auto-detect and convert to the other format.
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.{target}.safetensors).

## `sft lora extract`

Extract a LoRA adapter from the delta between base and fine-tuned models.

Computes low-rank approximation of (finetuned - base) weight deltas
using SVD decomposition.

Examples:
  sft lora extract base.safetensors finetuned.safetensors --rank 16
  sft lora extract base.safetensors ft.safetensors -r 64 -o adapter.safetensors
  sft lora extract base.safetensors ft.safetensors -r 32 --include='*self_attn*'

**Parameters:**

- `BASE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `FINETUNED` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--rank`/`-r` ‚Äî option, required. Target LoRA rank.
- `--output`/`-o` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {base_stem}.lora-r{rank}.safetensors).
- `--alpha` ‚Äî option. LoRA alpha for metadata (default: same as rank).
- `--include` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern for tensor names to include.
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern for tensor names to exclude.

## `sft lora info`

Analyze a LoRA adapter file.

Shows rank, alpha, target modules, layer count, and per-pair shapes.

Examples:
  sft lora info adapter.safetensors
  sft lora info adapter.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft lora merge`

Merge a LoRA adapter into a base model.

Applies W' = W + scale * (B @ A) for each target module.

Examples:
  sft lora merge base.safetensors adapter.safetensors
  sft lora merge base.safetensors adapter.safetensors --scale 0.5
  sft lora merge base.safetensors adapter.safetensors -o merged.safetensors --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `BASE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `ADAPTER` ‚Äî argument, required
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {base_stem}.merged.safetensors).
- `--scale` ‚Äî option. Override LoRA scale (default: alpha/rank from metadata, or 1.0).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show which tensors would be modified without writing.

## `sft lora resize`

Reduce LoRA rank via truncated SVD.

Keeps the top singular values/vectors, discarding the rest.

Examples:
  sft lora resize adapter.safetensors --rank 8
  sft lora resize adapter.safetensors -r 16 -o smaller.safetensors

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--rank`/`-r` ‚Äî option, required. Target rank (must be less than current rank).
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.r{rank}.safetensors).

## `sft lora svd`

Analyze the singular value spectrum of LoRA matrices.

Shows how many singular values are needed to capture 90/95/99% of
variance, helping decide if a lower rank is viable.

Examples:
  sft lora svd adapter.safetensors
  sft lora svd adapter.safetensors --threshold 0.99
  sft lora svd adapter.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--threshold`/`-t` ‚Äî option. Variance threshold for suggested rank (0.0‚Äì1.0).
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft ls`

Print a tabular summary of one or more .safetensors files.

Shows tensor count, parameter count, file size, and dtypes for each file.

Examples:
  sft ls model.safetensors
  sft ls *.safetensors --sort=size
  sft ls shard_*.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILES` ‚Äî argument, required (repeatable)
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON array.
- `--sort` ‚Äî option. Sort rows by: name, size, params, tensors.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft metadata`

View or edit metadata in a .safetensors file.

Without --set or --unset, prints existing metadata. With --set/--unset,
writes a new file with updated metadata.

Examples:
  sft metadata model.safetensors
  sft metadata model.safetensors --json
  sft metadata model.safetensors --set format=pt --set version=2.0
  sft metadata model.safetensors --unset format

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--set` ‚Äî option. Set a metadata key-value pair (key=value). Can be repeated.
- `--unset` ‚Äî option. Remove a metadata key. Can be repeated.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output metadata as JSON.
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.metadata.safetensors).

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft rename`

Rename tensor keys using regex substitution.

Each --sub takes a PATTERN and REPLACEMENT pair (Python regex syntax).
Multiple --sub options can be chained.

Examples:
  sft rename model.safetensors --sub 'model\.layers' 'layers' --dry-run
  sft rename model.safetensors --sub 'base_model\.model\.' ''
  sft rename model.safetensors --sub 'lora_A' 'lora_down' --sub 'lora_B' 'lora_up'

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--sub` ‚Äî option. Regex substitution: --sub PATTERN REPLACEMENT (can repeat).
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output path (default: {stem}.renamed.safetensors).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show old ‚Üí new name mappings without writing.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft skill install`

Install the sft agent skill for one or more agents.

Examples:
  sft skill install                            # auto-detect installed agents
  sft skill install --agent claude --agent cursor
  sft skill install --scope project            # install into ./.agents/skills/sft/
  sft skill install --mode copy --force

**Parameters:**

- `--agent`/`-a` ‚Äî option. Which agent(s) to install for. Repeatable, comma-separated, or 'all'/'auto'. Default: auto-detect installed agents (always includes ~/.agents/ for the open standard).
- `--scope`/`-s` ‚Äî option. 'user' (default, ~/...) or 'project' (./...).
- `--mode`/`-m` ‚Äî option. 'auto' (symlink ‚Üí junction ‚Üí copy), 'link' (force symlink), or 'copy'.
- `--force`/`-f` ‚Äî flag. Replace an existing install at the target.
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Print what would happen without changing anything.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Emit a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft skill path`

Print the resolved source skill directory inside the installed package.

**Parameters:**

- `--json` ‚Äî flag.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft skill status`

Show installation status for every known agent.

**Parameters:**

- `--scope`/`-s` ‚Äî option.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft skill uninstall`

Remove the sft skill from one or more agents.

**Parameters:**

- `--agent`/`-a` ‚Äî option. Which agent(s) to uninstall from. Same syntax as install.
- `--scope`/`-s` ‚Äî option.
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft skill update`

Refresh existing installs.

No-op for working symlinks/junctions (the target already points at the
current source). For copies, replaces the copy with a fresh one.

**Parameters:**

- `--agent`/`-a` ‚Äî option.
- `--scope`/`-s` ‚Äî option.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft slice`

Extract tensors matching a pattern into a new file.

Use --include to keep only matching tensors, --exclude to remove
matching tensors, or both to include first then exclude.

Examples:
  sft slice model.safetensors --include='**.weight'
  sft slice model.safetensors --exclude='**.bias' -o no_bias.safetensors
  sft slice model.safetensors --include='model.layers.0.**' --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--include` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern for tensor names to keep.
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern for tensor names to remove.
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output file path (default: {stem}.sliced.safetensors).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show what would be included/removed without writing.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft split`

Split a .safetensors file into smaller shards by size.

Creates numbered shard files and a JSON index file.
Size format: number followed by B, KB, MB, or GB.

Examples:
  sft split model.safetensors --max-size 4GB
  sft split model.safetensors --max-size 500MB --dry-run
  sft split model.safetensors --max-size 2GB -o 'shard-{index}.safetensors'

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--max-size` ‚Äî option, required. Maximum shard size (e.g. '4GB', '500MB', '1024B').
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output filename pattern (must contain '{index}'). Default: {stem}-{index}.safetensors
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show shard distribution without writing files.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft stat`

Compute per-tensor statistics (mean, std, min, max, sparsity, NaN/Inf).

Useful for sanity-checking model weights or detecting corruption.
Use --check in CI to fail on NaN/Inf values.

Examples:
  sft stat model.safetensors
  sft stat model.safetensors --include='*lora*' --json
  sft stat model.safetensors --check

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--include` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern to include tensors.
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option. Glob pattern to exclude tensors.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output as JSON.
- `--check` ‚Äî flag. Exit code 1 if any NaN or Inf found.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft strip`

Remove tensors matching a pattern from a file.

Glob patterns:
  *name*       matches any tensor containing "name"
  **.weight    matches tensors ending with ".weight" at any depth
  model.*.*    matches exactly two segments under "model"

Examples:
  sft strip model.safetensors --exclude='*lora_A*'
  sft strip model.safetensors --exclude='**.bias' -o slim.safetensors
  sft strip model.safetensors --exclude='*lora_A*' --dry-run

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--exclude` ‚Äî option, required. Glob pattern for tensor names to remove.
- `-o`/`--output` ‚Äî option. Output file path (default: {stem}.stripped.safetensors).
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî flag. Show what would be included/removed without writing.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output a JSON report.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).

## `sft tree`

Print tensor namespace hierarchy as an ASCII tree.

Tensor names are split on '.' to form a directory-like structure.

Examples:
  sft tree model.safetensors
  sft tree model.safetensors --depth 3
  sft tree model.safetensors --json

**Parameters:**

- `FILE` ‚Äî argument, required
- `--depth`/`-d` ‚Äî option. Limit tree depth.
- `--json` ‚Äî flag. Output the tree as JSON.

Supports `--json` (use this when parsing the output).
